title: HGU Media Analytics Platform
summary: Proof-of-concept analytics platform that centralizes paid media performance across ad platforms into one canonical daily campaign mart.
impact: Created a standardized cross-platform reporting model for executive dashboards and future optimization use cases.
Kept vendor API complexity upstream in Fivetran Quickstart dbt models while owning downstream canonical business logic.
Designed the POC so scaling from one client to all accounts requires configuration and data volume changes, not model refactors.
details: The canonical mart `mart_paid_media__campaign_daily` is modeled at date_day x platform x platform_account x campaign. Tests enforce non-null keys, accepted platform values, and grain consistency.

title: ProductDW
summary: Mini ELT and analytics engineering project that transforms GA4 sample event data into analytics-ready BigQuery tables and KPIs.
impact: Built a warehouse-native ELT flow from staging to core to marts.
Used SQL as version-controlled transformation logic and Python for parameterized BigQuery orchestration.
Delivered an interactive Streamlit KPI surface over modeled data.
details: The project emphasizes dimensional modeling, clean joins, incremental thinking, warehouse-native execution, and reproducible pipeline structure.

title: Burrito Shack Executive Marketing Dashboard
summary: React and D3 executive analytics experience for marketing performance, growth opportunities, and KPI drill-down.
impact: Prepared messy digital performance data with traceable data quality decisions.
Created executive and analyst paths through overview, market, channel, details, and insights views.
Added automatically generated month-over-month and year-over-year insight cards.
details: The dashboard includes global market/channel/date filters, channel ROI, revenue and ROAS time series, market ranking, row-level investigation, and insight generation.

title: 1 Hard Thing a Day
summary: A deployed FastAPI web app with a Postgres-backed challenge spinner, user submissions, and admin approval workflow.
impact: Shipped a full-stack app beyond local development with database hosting, Docker, secrets, HTTPS, and deployment health checks.
Designed moderation flow so user-submitted content can be reviewed before entering the live catalog.
Used deployment experience to strengthen product engineering fundamentals.
details: The randomizer returns either one full challenge or two half-challenges. Submissions land in a moderation table before approval moves them into the live item catalog.
